title: fraxinus americana var. subpubescens
intro: Fraxinus americana var. subpubescens, also known as the American green ash, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America, distinguished by its smooth, gray bark and sub-pubescent leaves. The bark and leaves of the American green ash have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus americana var. subpubescens have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's medicinal properties can be prepared through various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create topical ointments, salves, and creams for skin treatments.
